claim: Cross-check confirms the explorer claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implementation exists with proper scope separation (project vs external) and web/fetch tool support for external evidence. All project files match the explorer summaries.

evidence.summary: Evidence dispatch schema defines VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES (project, external, user, unknown) and implements demandRequestsExternalResearch() and externalEvidencePresent() functions to detect external research requirements and validate external evidence presence.
evidence.why: Core implementation confirms scope separation logic and external tool support claimed by explorer.

evidence.summary: Runtime implementation runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() includes boundary snapshot audit (buildBoundarySnapshot, diffBoundarySnapshots), execution policy with project_mutation forbidden, and boundary violation detection that becomes readiness blockers.
evidence.why: Confirms harness boundary audit prevents target-project mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code: false and explicit objectives for read-only evidence gathering and independent cross-check.
evidence.why: Verifies evidence agent protocols exist as claimed by explorer.

evidence.summary: Evidence scope inference functions (evidenceRecordScope, evidenceScopeSummary) classify evidence records into project/external/user/unknown scopes based on declared scope, URL presence, path presence, and source type prefixes.
evidence.why: Confirms automatic scope classification logic for evidence records.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() function generates blockers when evidence marked as project-scoped lacks repo-relative path, or when existing-project facts lack project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Verifies project evidence requires concrete path locators as claimed.

evidence.summary: Doctrine defines evidence agent protocols, scope requirements, and explicitly states: evidence records must declare scope as project, external, user, or unknown; project evidence requires repo-relative path or file locator; external evidence cannot alone prove existing-project facts.
evidence.why: Documentation confirms ground rules for evidence quality and scope separation.

evidence.summary: Example Domain documentation page fetched successfully confirms this is a documentation example domain as specified in the demand text.
evidence.why: External research tool availability confirmed and URL matches demand description.

evidence.summary: Evidence directory structure exists at state root with 252 entries, confirming artifact storage system is in place.
evidence.why: Verifies evidence system infrastructure exists as claimed by explorer.

risks: External web evidence could be mistaken for project implementation facts without proper scope=external labeling
Web/fetch tools might not be available in all runtime environments
Evidence agents might mix project-scoped path evidence with external URL evidence

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all explorer claims. Evidence dispatch implementation exists with VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES including project and external, automatic scope classification logic, boundary mutation detection, and web/fetch tool support. The demand example URL (https://example.com) was successfully fetched. Ready for verifier to confirm blockers and assumptions are properly separated from facts.
